memoryArtifacts.headline:
Alicia Hustings Clark
(1490 – after 31 August 1554)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Alice, wife of Robert Clark, was probably born about 1495 in Norfolk County, England. 

Her parentage is unknown. 

She married Robert about 1518, probably in Banham, Norfolk, England. 

They had at l …

zh.lifeSummary: Alice Mack於從1490年到1495年出生在英國諾福爾可班納姆。她於大約1519年與Robert Williamson Clarke在英國諾福爾可諾斯 伍頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1554年8月在她的家鄉，埋葬在英國諾福爾可班納姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alice Mack于从1490年到1495年出生在英国諾福爾可班納姆。她于大约1519年与Robert Williamson Clarke在英国諾福爾可諾斯 伍頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1554年8月在她的家乡，埋葬在英国諾福爾可班納姆。

nameMeanings.name: Mack
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: from the Old Norse personal name Makkr, representing an Old Irish name Mac ‘son’ Latinized as Maccus. It was once thought that Maccus was a form of Magnus , but this is not so.

Scottish and Irish: possibly from a shortened form of any of the many Scottish and Irish surnames beginning with Mac- ‘son’, a nickname that occasionally became encoded as a nickname and thence perhaps as a genuine surname, but the evidence for this is thin.

North German and Dutch: from the ancient Germanic personal name Macco, Makko, a pet form of a compound name with the first element māg- ‘kinsman’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the much more common Dutch variant Mak .

variants.name: Mackes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt.
North German: patronymic from the personal name   Mack   3.

variants.name: Macke
meanings: German: variant of   Mack   3.

variants.name: Meck
meanings: German: from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Macco (see Mack 3).

variants.name: Magnus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Bernd, Egon, Erwin, Kurt, Otto, Reinhard, Volker, Wilhelm, Wolfgang.

German, Flemish, and Walloon: from the personal name Magnus, which is from the Latin adjective magnus ‘great’ or a Latinized form of a short form of ancient Germanic compound names based on the element magan ‘strength, might’. The name was borne by several early Christian saints, including St. Magnus (8th century), a monk in St. Gallen in Switzerland and missionary in Allgäu in southern Germany, whose real name was Magnoald. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). In Belgium, it may also be a Latinized (humanistic) form of Walloon Legrand .

English, Scottish, Swedish, Danish, Norwegian, and North German: from the Scandinavian personal name Magnus, borne by Magnus the Good (died 1047), king of Norway and Denmark, who was named for the Emperor Charlemagne, Latin Carolus Magnus ‘Charles the Great’, under the impression that Magnus was a personal name. The name spread from Norway to the eastern Scandinavian royal houses, and became popular all over Scandinavia and thence in the English Danelaw. It has also been adopted as a Jewish name.

variants.name: Mackel
meanings: German: from a pet form of Mack 3 or a related personal name such as Markward or Markhart (Mackart).
English (Durham) and Irish (Derry): variant of   Mackle  .

variants.name: Mook
meanings: Dutch (also Van Mook): habitational name for someone from a place called Mook, in Dutch Limburg.
German: variant of   Mack  ,   Mock  .
English (Yorkshire): variant of   Mock  .

variants.name: Mages
meanings: German: probably a patronymic from a variant of the personal name   Mack   3.
English (Norfolk): apparently a variant of   Maggs  .
History: The family of Wolfgang Mages (1784–1871) emigrated from Freihung, Bavaria, to Milwaukee, WI, in the early 19th century.

variants.name: Macks
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of Mack , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.
Americanized form of German Max 1 (compare Max 2) or Mack 3 (with the addition of excrescent -s, a common feature of Americanized surnames), or perhaps of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Maack
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Horst, Kurt, Otto, Uwe.
North German: variant of   Mack   3.

variants.name: Mackle
meanings: South German (Mäckle): from a pet form of Mack 3.

Irish (Roscommon): perhaps from the patronymic Ó Machail, ‘descendant of Machal’. It has sometimes been altered to McKell , as if it were etymologically a Mc- patronymic. Occasionally also a synonym for McGill in the county of Down.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: Alice Mack was born from 1490 to 1495, in Banham, Norfolk, England. She married Robert Williamson Clarke about 1519, in North Wootton, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died in August 1554, in her hometown, and was buried in Banham, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Alice Mack est née du 1490 au 1495, à Banham, Norfolk, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Robert Williamson Clarke environ 1519, à North Wootton, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en août 1554, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterrée à Banham, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Alice Mack wurde von 1490 bis 1495 in Banham, Norfolk, England geboren. Sie hat um 1519 in North Wootton, Norfolk, England Robert Williamson Clarke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb im August 1554 in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Banham, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Alice Mack è nata il da 1490 a 1495 nel Banham, Norfolk, England. Lei sposato Robert Williamson Clarke circa 1519, nel North Wootton, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel agosto 1554, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolta a Banham, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Mackはから1490年まで1495年にイギリスノーフォルクバンハムで生まれた。約1519年にイギリスノーフォルクノース ウットオンでRobert Williamson Clarkeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1554年8月に故郷で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクバンハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Mack(은)는 1490년 부터 1495년 까지 영국 노르폴크 반함에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1519년 영국 노르폴크 노르스 보턴에서 Robert Williamson Clarke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1554년 8월 그녀의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 반함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alice Mack nasceu de 1490 a 1495, em Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Robert Williamson Clarke aproximadamente 1519, em North Wootton, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em agosto de 1554, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultada em Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Alice Mack родилась с 1490 по 1495 в местности банам, норфолк, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Williamson Clarke около 1519 г. в местности норт вуттон, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла август 1554 г. в её родном городе и была похоронена в местности банам, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Alice Mack nació desde 1490 hasta 1495, en Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Se casó con Robert Williamson Clarke aproximadamente 1519, en North Wootton, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en agosto de 1554, en su pueblo, y fue sepultada en Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
